findings.notes: Single dependency-free CommonJS function that lowercases, trims, collapses non-alphanumeric runs to hyphens, strips edge hyphens, and caps length; import and call directly.
[HARD] Sole export; consumers integrate via require('./slugify') and receive the function value directly (no named/default namespace).
Fully self-contained — zero external or transitive dependencies, no I/O and no global state, safe to vendor or copy verbatim.
ES5-era style (strict pragma, var, function declaration, chained .replace(); no const/let/arrow) — match this style when extending in place.
Optional second arg is defaulted via || 60 fallback rather than a real default parameter, so the effective default is 60.
Length cap slices AFTER edge-hyphen stripping, so a cut landing on a hyphen boundary can leave a trailing hyphen, violating spec.md req 4/5 (no edge separators, no broken cut).
Only number-vs-non-number is handled; null/undefined/object/boolean input reaches .toLowerCase() on line 15 and throws TypeError, contradicting tasks.md:7 'Input validation complete'.
maxLen || 60 coerces a legitimate falsy 0 back to 60 and does not guard a negative maxLen (slice would then count from the string end).
Non-ASCII/accented characters are dropped, not transliterated ('café' -> 'caf', not 'cafe'); matches the spec.md example but limits multilingual reuse.
All-non-alphanumeric input (e.g. '!!!') yields an empty slug; spec.md defines no behavior for an empty result, an unresolved contract ambiguity for callers using slugs as identifiers.
